papers.title: Inter‐platform ecosystems
papers.abstract: We extend ecosystem theory to cases in which platforms are complementors to each other: inter-platform ecosystems. Analyzing web traffic data on 241 European platforms, we identify and characterize demand-side inter-platform ecosystems, and propose a theory of why they emerge. We posit that demand-side inter-platform ecosystems solve matching problems generated by externalities platforms impose on each other. We describe four strategies platforms implement to solve these problems: network fusion (hosting competitor content), user-community-driven interactions (facilitating cross-posting), meta-platform (aggregating another platform), and platform concatenation (referring users to another platform for customized complementary services).
papers.summary_ja: 241のヨーロッパプラットフォームのウェブトラフィックデータを用い、複数プラットフォームが互いにコンプリメンターとして機能する「インタープラットフォーム・エコシステム」を発見し、ネットワーク融合・ユーザーコミュニティ主導・メタプラットフォーム・プラットフォーム連結という4戦略類型を提示した実証研究。

papers.title: Unlocking novel knowledge recombinations: The effect of artificial intelligence on innovation
papers.abstract: We examine whether artificial intelligence (AI) unlocks novel knowledge recombinations and how AI usage influences innovation. Using a dataset of 21,000+ firms across 41 countries, we find that AI usage is positively associated with innovation. This relationship is mediated by novel knowledge recombinations, i.e., AI enables firms to discover and recombine knowledge elements that were previously difficult to combine, which in turn facilitates innovation.
papers.summary_ja: 41カ国2万1千社超のデータを用い、AIが「これまで困難だった知識の新規組み合わせ」を媒介してイノベーションを促進することを大規模実証した、AI×イノベーション戦略研究の重要論文。

papers.title: Investigating the use of design thinking in identifying wicked problems of startups
papers.abstract: Design thinking is defined as using strategies from the field of design to solve problems in the disciplines that are traditionally not considered design. This research explores the utilization of design thinking in identifying and refining the wicked problems that often precipitate failure. Over 18 months, we examined five startups applying the Research through Design (RtD) method and Double Diamond framework (DDF) in 4 discovery, define, develop, and deliver stages. Findings demonstrate a notable misalignment between startup offerings and market requirements, predominantly due to the misdefinition of problems applying design thinking.
papers.summary_ja: 18ヶ月間5社のスタートアップを対象に、デザイン思考（ダブルダイアモンドフレームワーク）がウィキッドプロブレムの同定・リファインに有効な一方、問題定義の誤りが事業失敗の主因となることをResearch through Design法で実証した研究。

papers.title: Triggers of sustainable business model innovation in technology-based startups: An empirical investigation
papers.abstract: This study examines what technology-based startups treat as external triggers for initiating sustainable business model innovation (SBMI) through 46 semi-structured interviews of 31 sustainability-oriented startups in the Netherlands. The analysis identifies seven trigger domains across two levels: value network level and institutional level.
papers.summary_ja: オランダの持続可能性志向スタートアップ31社46インタビューから、サステナブルBMIを誘発する外部トリガーをバリューネットワーク・制度レベルの2層7ドメインに整理した実証研究。

papers.title: The digital paradox in social innovation: the interplay between digital maturity and business model innovation
papers.abstract: This study analyses how digital maturity influence companies' ability to develop social innovation and explores the moderating role of business model innovation (BMI). Results show a negative moderating effect of BMI on the relationship between digital maturity and social innovation, suggesting organisational tensions from simultaneous digital and structural transformations—ambidexterity challenges.
papers.summary_ja: デジタル成熟度がソーシャルイノベーション創出能力を高める一方、ビジネスモデルイノベーション（BMI）は両者の関係を負に調整する「デジタルパラドックス」を実証し、同時並行的なデジタル変革とBMIが両利き経営の緊張をもたらすことを示した研究。

papers.title: Applying Design Science in Creativity and Innovation Management Research: A Decision Tree
papers.abstract: This paper presents a decision tree for applying design science methodology in creativity and innovation management research. The framework guides researchers in selecting appropriate design science approaches and artefacts for different research questions in the creativity and innovation management domain.
papers.summary_ja: 創造性・イノベーション管理研究においてデザインサイエンス手法を適切に選択・適用するための意思決定ツリーを提案し、研究目的に応じたアーティファクト設計の指針を与えた方法論論文。

papers.title: Information Dynamics and Learning in Complex Adaptive Systems: Toward a Transdisciplinary Framework
papers.authors: Anderson de Souza Sant'Anna et al.
papers.abstract: This article develops a framework for understanding learning and adaptation in complex adaptive systems. Drawing from neuroscience, systems theory, information theory and quantum field theory, it examines how information processing, plasticity and systemic coherence emerge from distributed, nonlinear and feedback-driven interactions. It identifies isomorphisms linking synaptic plasticity to organizational learning, neural connectivity to communication networks and phase transitions to innovation tipping points.
papers.summary_ja: 神経科学・システム理論・情報理論・量子場理論を横断し、複雑適応系における情報処理・可塑性・システム的整合性の生成メカニズムを解明、シナプス可塑性と組織学習の同形性を示す学際的フレームワークを提案した論文。

papers.title: Unknowing the future: Speculative foresight in international institutions and the unexpected 'origins' of expertise
papers.abstract: This article explores speculative foresight as a form of future-oriented expertise in international institutions, tapping into art, literature, management philosophy, and geopolitics. Foresight is authorized through claims to innovation, imagination, pluralism, and methodological correctness, and differs from forecasting by imagining the future as contingent, plural, and unknowable.
papers.summary_ja: 国際機関における「思弁的フォーサイト（Speculative Foresight）」の実践を知識政治学の視点から分析し、未来を「未知・複数・偶発的なもの」として問い直す方法論的権威がどのように構成されるかを解明した論文。

papers.title: Integrating knowledge management and digital product passports to foster sustainable and collaborative ecosystems
papers.abstract: This study explores the potential of the digital product passport (DPP) to support circular and collaborative ecosystems in the fashion and cosmetics industries. The DPP is examined as an integrative tool embedding sustainability, open innovation (OI) and knowledge management into organizational practice.
papers.summary_ja: デジタルプロダクトパスポート（DPP）がファッション・化粧品業界のサーキュラーエコノミー・コラボレーティブエコシステムを支援する可能性を探り、DPP・オープンイノベーション・知識管理を統合した組織実践フレームを提案した研究。

papers.title: Resource redeployment in multi‐business firms: Centralized or decentralized?
papers.abstract: We examine when resource redeployment in multi-business firms should be centralized vs. decentralized. We argue that centralization is more beneficial when resources are more fungible (easily redeployed), when the firm faces greater business unit heterogeneity, and when it operates in more dynamic environments. Decentralization is more beneficial when resources are more specific to business units and when information asymmetries between headquarters and units are greater.
papers.summary_ja: 多事業企業がリソース再配分を集権・分権のいずれで行うべきかを、リソース代替可能性・事業多様性・環境ダイナミズムの3要因から規定した戦略論の実証研究。
